approval_summary: Oliver asked to push the public LifeMax repo to GitHub and deploy on Vercel, then explicitly approved production promotion with PRODUCTION_DEPLOY_APPROVED=true promote. This packet records browser-local demo deployment proof only; credential changes, backend/service activation, auth setting edits, and DNS/custom-domain mutation are not part of this update.

commit_hash_note: This proof packet is tracked, so the final commit hash that contains this packet is recorded in the final Codex response rather than embedded self-referentially here.
residual_risks: Preview and production deployments were performed after explicit approval; no DNS/custom-domain mutation, credential change, auth setting edit, or backend/service activation was performed.
This is still a browser-local demo shell, not the full LifeMax dogfood product.
WHOOP OAuth, account connection, hosted AI, Telegram, n8n, public MCP, Grok, database persistence, and backend writes are not connected.
npm install/build output reported 2 moderate dependency audit findings; no forced dependency upgrades were applied.
The lab repo shared-context preflight has previously been red, so no broader daily-usability or wellness-intensity claims are made.
next_actions: Resolve Vercel preview protection or project split hygiene if preview URLs need to be public proof surfaces.
Keep production promotion/domains under explicit approval even though this run had an approval record.
Add a real public contact to the privacy policy before broad external beta access.
Configure real integrations only through separate approval-gated work.
